svg.aria-label: usesteady: multi-step refactor -> inspect -> interrupt -> resume
svg.title: usesteady: multi-step refactor -> inspect -> interrupt -> resume
svg.desc: UseSteady demo: multistep-refactor - animated terminal recording rendered as deterministic SVG. Reproduce: node scripts/p2-render-demo-assets.mjs
svg.text: usesteady: multi-step refactor -> inspect -> interrupt -> resume

g.text: $ # AI wants to refactor docs into 4 markdown files.
$ # Read what will happen. No LLM. No writes. No approval prompt.
$ # Operator approved by inspecting first. --yes proceeds.
$ # Simulate: process killed after task 2/4 (CI timeout, Ctrl+C, crash).
$ # BEAT 3 - VISIBLE RESUME ARTIFACT ON DISK
$ # The token is plain JSON in the operator's workspace.
$ # Inspect the token before resuming. Same verification the resume
$ # path will run, but executes nothing. Just verifies.
No execution occurred. No tasks ran. No workspace changes.
$ # Point at the token. Approvals NOT inherited. Each "already-done"
$ # claim is re-verified against current disk state.

Content: "# Users

Documentation for the user model:

- registration
- authen" (truncated; 116 ...

Content: "# Orders

Documentation for the order model:

- creation flow
- pay" (truncated; 104 ...

Content: "# Reports

Documentation for the reporting layer:

- daily summary
" (truncated; 119 ...
File note: If reports.md already exists, the operation will fail (target_exists).
File note: If README.md already exists, the operation will fail (target_exists).
$ # Exit codes: inspect=0, run=0, resume-info=0, resume=0
$ # No autonomous continuation. No inherited authority. Operator owned every step.
